result.stdout:
ReleaseCraft - Automated Release Notes Generator

Usage:
  ReleaseCraft --owner <owner> --repo <repo> --from-tag <tag> --to-tag <tag> [options]
  ReleaseCraft --help

Required Arguments:
  --owner, -o <value>          GitHub repository owner (username or organization)
                               Can be set in .releasecraftrc.json config file
  --repo, -r <value>           GitHub repository name
                               Can be set in .releasecraftrc.json config file
  --from-tag, -f <value>       Starting tag for comparison
  --to-tag, -t <value>         Ending tag for comparison

Optional Arguments:
  --token <value>              GitHub personal access token (PAT)
                               If not provided, reads from GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable
  --output <path>              Output file path (if not specified, writes to console)
                               Can be set in .releasecraftrc.json config file
  --format <format>            Output format for release notes
                               Values: markdown (default), json, html, csv
                               Example: --format json
  --audience <mode>            Target audience mode for tone control
                               Values: client (non-technical), internal (technical, default)
                               Example: --audience client
  --ai                         Enable AI-powered summarization and enhancement
                               Requires OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable
  --tone <style>               AI tone style for generated content (requires --ai)
                               Values: professional (default), casual, marketing, technical
                               Example: --tone marketing
  --no-config                  Disable config file loading
  --help, -h                   Show this help message

Configuration File:
  ReleaseCraft looks for .releasecraftrc.json in:
  1. Current directory (./.releasecraftrc.json)
  2. Repository root (if in git repo)
  3. User home directory (~/.releasecraftrc.json)
  
  Example .releasecraftrc.json:
  {
    "owner": "microsoft",
    "repo": "vscode",
    "outputPath": "RELEASE_NOTES.md",
    "defaultCategory": "Other Changes",
    "ai": {
      "enabled": true,
      "apiKey": "env:OPENAI_API_KEY",
      "model": "gpt-4-turbo",
      "tone": "professional",
      "features": {
        "summarization": true,
        "toneControl": true
      }
    }
  }
  
  Note: CLI arguments always override config file values.

Examples:
  # Basic usage
  dotnet run -- --owner microsoft --repo vscode --from-tag 1.84.0 --to-tag 1.85.0
  
  # Generate JSON output
  dotnet run -- --owner microsoft --repo vscode --from-tag 1.84.0 --to-tag 1.85.0 --format json --output release-notes.json
  
  # Generate HTML output
  dotnet run -- --owner microsoft --repo vscode --from-tag 1.84.0 --to-tag 1.85.0 --format html --output release-notes.html
  
  # Generate CSV output
  dotnet run -- --owner microsoft --repo vscode --from-tag 1.84.0 --to-tag 1.85.0 --format csv --output release-notes.csv
  
  # With AI summarization (professional tone)
  dotnet run -- --owner microsoft --repo vscode --from-tag 1.84.0 --to-tag 1.85.0 --ai
  
  # With AI and marketing tone
  dotnet run -- --owner microsoft --repo vscode --from-tag 1.84.0 --to-tag 1.85.0 --ai --tone marketing
  
  # Client audience mode (non-technical)
  dotnet run -- --owner microsoft --repo vscode --from-tag 1.84.0 --to-tag 1.85.0 --audience client
  
  # Combined: AI + marketing tone + client audience + JSON format
  dotnet run -- --owner microsoft --repo vscode --from-tag 1.84.0 --to-tag 1.85.0 --ai --tone marketing --audience client --format json
  
  # Using config file
  dotnet run -- --from-tag v0.1 --to-tag v0.2    # Uses owner/repo from config file
  
  # Show help
  dotnet run -- --help
